skill.name: skill-developer
skill.description: Guides creation, configuration, testing, and troubleshooting of Claude Code skills, triggers, guardrails, hooks, and progressive disclosure.
skill.summary: Build and troubleshoot Claude Code skills with triggers, guardrails, hooks, and reference documentation.
skill.author: diet103

security_audit.summary: AI review found the static findings to be false positives from markdown documentation, shell examples, and configuration references. No executable files, credential exfiltration, malicious network calls, or prompt injection attempts were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: External Command Examples In Documentation
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The external command detections are fenced shell examples and hook testing commands in markdown documentation. They are not hidden executable source code, and the skill package contains no script that runs them automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are markdown examples for testing hooks. They require explicit user action and are consistent with the documented troubleshooting purpose.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto And Sensitive Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak cryptography and sensitive file detections match words such as Markdown file names, DESCRIBE command text, test framework describe calls, and skill keyword fields. No cryptographic implementation or certificate material is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows documentation text and example field names, not crypto code or secret-bearing files. The static pattern is overbroad for markdown content.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hardcoded Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The network detections are references to a public regex testing site used for validating patterns. The skill does not perform network requests or transmit data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only as human-facing references in markdown. There is no fetch, curl, webhook, or automated network operation in the skill files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hidden File And Reconnaissance References
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. References to .claude paths, ls, cat, jq, chmod, and npm install are troubleshooting instructions for local hook configuration. They do not indicate covert filesystem access or host reconnaissance by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible troubleshooting steps for a documented Claude Code hook system. No file-reading code is bundled with this documentation-only skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Combined Obfuscation Heuristic
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The critical heuristic combined separate markdown examples for commands, URLs, and local paths. Review found no obfuscation, no credential collection, and no data exfiltration path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The risky categories occur in separate explanatory sections and are not linked by executable code. The package intent is skill-authoring documentation.

content.value_statement: Skill authors need clear rules for reliable activation and guardrails. This skill explains triggers, hooks, testing, and troubleshooting for Claude Code workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explain Claude Code skill structure, frontmatter, descriptions, and reference files
Configure keyword, intent, file path, and content triggers for skill activation
Describe block, suggest, and warn enforcement levels for different guardrail needs
Guide setup concepts for UserPromptSubmit and PreToolUse hook behavior
Provide troubleshooting steps for missed triggers, false positives, and hook failures
Document progressive disclosure and the 500-line rule for maintainable skills
limitations: It is documentation-only and does not create files by itself
It assumes a Claude Code project with compatible hook configuration
It does not replace security review of custom hook scripts
It focuses on Claude Code skills, with limited direct Codex-specific setup guidance

use_cases.title: Create a first skill
use_cases.description: Learn the required structure, frontmatter, trigger language, and testing steps for a new skill.

use_cases.title: Design team guardrails
use_cases.description: Plan blocking or advisory checks that help teams avoid repeated implementation mistakes.

use_cases.title: Troubleshoot activation
use_cases.description: Diagnose missing suggestions, unexpected blocks, broad patterns, and broken hook registration.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a Claude Code skill for database schema work. Include the purpose, trigger terms, and a testing checklist.

prompt_templates.title: Tune Triggers
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my planned skill triggers for false positives and false negatives. Suggest clearer keywords and intent patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Guardrail
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose between block, suggest, and warn for a skill that prevents unsafe database edits.

prompt_templates.title: Debug Hook Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Walk me through diagnosing a Claude Code skill that does not activate during file edits or user prompts.

output_examples.input: I need a skill that activates for database migration work.
output: Recommended trigger terms include database, migration, schema, table, and column.
Use advisory suggestions for learning workflows and blocking guardrails for risky edits.
Test prompts should include both direct requests and indirect feature requests.

output_examples.input: My skill triggers too often when users say create.
output: Replace broad action words with domain-specific phrases.
Add nouns that identify the real workflow, such as component, endpoint, or schema.
Test several unrelated prompts to confirm the skill stays quiet.

output_examples.input: How should I split a long skill document?
output: Keep the main skill file focused on purpose, usage, and quick steps.
Move long references into separate files with clear names.
Add a table of contents when a reference file becomes long.

best_practices: Use specific trigger terms and test them with real user prompts before publishing.
Reserve blocking enforcement for issues that can cause real defects or data risk.
Keep the main skill concise and move deep reference material into linked files.
anti_patterns: Avoid single-word triggers that match unrelated work, such as create or system.
Do not use blocking hooks for general style preferences or optional guidance.
Do not place every detail in the main skill file when reference files are clearer.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps design Claude Code skills with clear descriptions, triggers, guardrails, hooks, and troubleshooting steps.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It is documentation-only and includes command examples for users to run manually when needed.

faq.question: Which trigger types does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers keyword triggers, intent patterns, file path patterns, and content patterns.

faq.question: When should I use block enforcement?
faq.answer: Use block enforcement only for critical guardrails that prevent likely defects, data loss, or unsafe edits.

faq.question: How does it support troubleshooting?
faq.answer: It lists checks for missed triggers, JSON syntax issues, broad patterns, hook registration, and slow hooks.

faq.question: Can Codex users use this skill?
faq.answer: Codex users can use the concepts, but some hook details are specific to Claude Code.
